description_long: This object is a dark, elongated stone with a roughly leaf-shaped outline and a pointed end, resembling a primitive hand tool or a large stone flake. The surface is uneven and marked by subtle ridges and flake scars, indicating it may have been shaped or chipped by human hands. The coloration is predominantly deep gray to black, with some lighter streaks and patches, and a slightly matte, rough finish. One side of the stone features a visible marking or catalog number in white, suggesting it has been part of a collection or study. The stone is relatively thin in profile, tapering to a sharp edge along its length, and appears solid and dense. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A dark, leaf-shaped stone with a rough, chipped surface and pointed end, featuring subtle ridges and a matte finish. It is thin, solid, and marked with a white catalog number on one side.
description_view_0: The broad, flat face of the stone is visible, showing its leaf-like shape, rough texture, and dark coloration.
description_view_1: The thin profile of the stone is evident, highlighting its sharp taper and narrow edge.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face is shown, featuring a white catalog number and similar rough surface details.
description_view_3: Another side profile view, emphasizing the stone's slenderness and uneven edge.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or garden path
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular flake scars and subtle veining, with a hand-chipped appearance and a small area of white marking.
attribute_weathering: Rough and matte, with minor surface wear but no significant erosion or lichen growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but can blend with natural stone arrangements or gravel beds.
